title: Flavorful Syrian Turkey Neck Stew
description: A winey, molten, hangover-cure Syrian dish featuring turkey neck, pickle, and Jain-friendly ingredients, this stew is perfect for a comforting dinner. It is a traditional Syrian recipe with a twist to cater to Jain dietary preferences.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the turkey neck and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped pickle, red wine, vegetable broth, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the turkey is tender.
Remove the turkey from the stew and let it cool, then shred it into bite-sized pieces and return it to the stew.
Simmer the stew for another 30 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the stew has thickened slightly.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
